authorities.name: California Department of Fish and Wildlife

reaches.name: Butte Creek Reach A (Oro-Chico Road bridge to Centerville Head Dam)
reaches.fromDesc: Oro-Chico Road bridge crossing south of Chico
reaches.toDesc: Centerville Head Dam, 300 yards downstream from the DeSabla Powerhouse below DeSabla Reservoir

reaches.name: Butte Creek Reach B (Oro-Chico Road bridge to Sacramento River via Butte Slough/Sutter Bypass/Sacramento Slough)
reaches.fromDesc: Oro-Chico Road bridge crossing south of Chico
reaches.toDesc: Confluence with the Sacramento River, via the Butte Slough outfall gates at Moon's Bend and via Butte Slough, the East and West Canals of the Sutter Bypass, and Sacramento Slough

sources.title: 14 CCR §7.40(b)(23) — Butte Creek (Butte and Sutter Cos.)
sources.quotedText: (23) Butte Creek (Butte and Sutter Cos.). (A) From the Oro-Chico Road bridge crossing south of Chico to the Centerville Head Dam, located 300 yards downstream from the DeSabla Powerhouse below DeSabla Reservoir. Nov. 15 through Feb. 15. Only artificial lures with barbless hooks may be used. 2 hatchery trout or hatchery steelhead **. 4 hatchery trout or hatchery steelhead ** in possession. (B) From the Oro-Chico Road bridge crossing south of Chico to the point that Butte Creek enters the Sacramento River both via Butte Slough outfall gates at Moon's Bend and through Butte Slough, thence both the East and West Canals of the Sutter Bypass, thence Sacramento Slough. All year. Open to fishing for non-salmonids only. Closed to the take of trout and steelhead.

sources.title: 14 CCR §7.40(a) — General provisions and footnote definitions for the anadromous/hatchery special-regulations water list
sources.quotedText: Every body of water listed in subsection (b) is closed to the take of hatchery trout, hatchery steelhead, and salmon and to fishing for these species, unless otherwise noted. ... Hatchery trout or steelhead in anadromous waters are those showing a healed adipose fin clip (adipose fin is absent). Unless otherwise provided, all other trout and steelhead must be immediately released.

sources.title: Fishing Regulations — Friends of Butte Creek
California Fish and Game Code §7145 — Sport Fishing License Requirement
sources.quotedText: Except as otherwise provided in this article, every person 16 years of age or older who takes any fish, reptile, or amphibian for any purpose other than profit shall first obtain a valid license for that purpose...

regulations.humanSummary: Oro-Chico Road bridge to Centerville Head Dam: open November 15 through February 15.
Oro-Chico Road bridge to Centerville Head Dam: only artificial lures with barbless hooks may be used.
regulations.verbatimText: Only artificial lures with barbless hooks may be used.

parameters.counting_rule: Applies only to hatchery trout or hatchery steelhead — fish with a healed, missing adipose fin. All other (unclipped/wild) trout and steelhead must be released unharmed per the statewide §7.40(a) hatchery-fish definition.

regulations.humanSummary: 2 hatchery trout or hatchery steelhead daily, 4 in possession, Oro-Chico Road bridge to Centerville Head Dam.
regulations.verbatimText: 2 hatchery trout or hatchery steelhead **. 4 hatchery trout or hatchery steelhead ** in possession.

parameters.condition: Trout or steelhead without a healed adipose fin clip (wild/unmarked fish)
parameters.note: Only hatchery-marked (adipose fin-clipped) trout or steelhead may be retained; all other trout and steelhead must be released unharmed.

regulations.humanSummary: Only adipose fin-clipped hatchery trout/steelhead may be kept; wild (unclipped) trout and steelhead must be released unharmed.
regulations.verbatimText: Hatchery trout or steelhead in anadromous waters are those showing a healed adipose fin clip (adipose fin is absent). Unless otherwise provided, all other trout and steelhead must be immediately released.

regulations.humanSummary: Oro-Chico Road bridge downstream to the Sacramento River (via Butte Slough outfall gates and the Sutter Bypass/Sacramento Slough): open all year for non-salmonid species.

parameters.note: Closed to the take of trout and steelhead year-round in this lower reach; open only for non-salmonid species.

regulations.humanSummary: Closed year-round to the take of trout and steelhead from the Oro-Chico Road bridge downstream to the Sacramento River; only non-salmonid fishing is open.
regulations.verbatimText: Open to fishing for non-salmonids only. Closed to the take of trout and steelhead.

parameters.note: Anglers 16 years of age or older must have a valid California sport fishing license.

regulations.humanSummary: California sport fishing license required for anglers 16+
regulations.verbatimText: Except as otherwise provided in this article, every person 16 years of age or older who takes any fish, reptile, or amphibian for any purpose other than profit shall first obtain a valid license for that purpose...
